summary: The Oppo K14 5G is set to launch in India on March 9 at 12 noon. The phone will come with a 7,000 mAh battery ad support for 45W of fast charging. The device could come in the sub- ₹25,000 price bracket.&nbsp;

title: ‘Not worth it’: OpenAI researcher questions Pentagon amid ‘Cancel ChatGPT’ trend
summary: An OpenAI researcher has expressed concerns about the company's recent deal to deploy AI tools for the US Department of Defense. The company has been facing backlash since it announced the partnership with Pentagon last week.

title: Nothing Phone 4a, 4a pro price and storage variants leaked ahead of launch: here's what to expect
summary: A new leak has suggested that the Nothing Phone 4a could come with a 1.5K OLED display, Snapdragon 7s Gen 4, and a 50MP camera. The phone could begin at a price of ₹31,999 while the &nbsp;4a Pro could start at ₹40,000.

title: iPhone 17e vs Galaxy S25 FE vs Pixel 10a: Specs, features, India pricing and more compared
summary: Apple launched the iPhone 17e with an A19 processor, priced under ₹70,000. Competing with Pixel 10a and Galaxy S25 FE, it features a 6.1-inch display, IP68 rating, and a single 48MP camera. The Pixel 10a offers better battery and price, while the S25 FE has a more versatile camera setup.

summary: &nbsp;The device features the A19 chip and MagSafe support. Pre-orders begin Wednesday, with sales starting March 11, as Apple aims to attract new users amid rising memory chip prices.

title: Cursor’s annualised revenue crosses $2 billion mark in February: Report
summary: In November 2025, the AI coding startup had raised $2.3 billion at a valuation of $29.3 billion. In the last three months, sales have doubled, with nearly 60% of revenue now coming from corporate customers.

title: Karnataka renews Uber’s ride-hailing licence; signals hope for other players
summary: The licence renewal under the 2016 aggregator rules, which comes after years of correspondence and uncertainty, brings regulatory certainty to Uber’s business in Karnataka. Per industry watchers, this will restore confidence in the app-based mobility ecosystem.

title: Execution is the new frontier: How Anthropic, Perplexity and Moltbook are reshaping AI
summary: AI’s frontier has moved from beyond intelligence to orchestration. Autonomous agents, not just chatbots, are becoming the industry’s new power centre. Anthropic, Perplexity, and Moltbook are defining how autonomous systems will operate at scale.

title: EU experts to start work on social media ban for children Thursday
summary: An EU expert group is to begin work this week on whether to ban social media for children with the aim of coming up with recommendations by the summer, Brussels said Tuesday. title: Rapido’s food delivery app Ownly launches services across Bengaluru
summary: According to the company, Ownly is a zero-commission platform, i.e., it doesn’t charge any fee from restaurants. On the other hand, Zomato and Swiggy charge commissions ranging between 16-30%.

title: Apple launches new MacBooks with M5 chips, bigger base storage
summary: Apple has launched new MacBook Air and MacBook Pro models. These feature the latest M5 chips and offer more storage as standard. This move aims to attract buyers in a challenging PC market. title: Singapore expands foreign workforce, to launch new pass for tech talent
summary: Singapore is opening eight service sector jobs to workers from India and other countries starting September. A new work pass will be introduced in 2027 to attract global talent in emerging technologies. This move aims to help businesses hire more workers and retain skilled foreign talent.

title: Diagnostics startup Droplet Biosciences partners with Nvidia to speed cancer test results
summary: Droplet Biosciences is partnering with Nvidia. They will use Nvidia's AI technology to speed up cancer test results after surgery. title: Tata Group prepared to mitigate risks from West Asia: Chairman Chandrasekaran
summary: Tata Group Chairman N Chandrasekaran hopes the West Asia conflict will not impact supply chains. The conglomerate has risk mitigation plans and prioritises employee safety. Tata Steel is diversifying limestone sources.
